data.title: AI may be the key to success in banking, as Wall Street banks face competition from peers integrating AI effectively.
data.description: A recent report by Bank of America highlights the significance of AI in the banking sector, suggesting that it could be a decisive factor separating successful institutions from struggling ones in the coming years. The report emphasizes that traditional banks are not primarily threatened by AI startups, but by competitors who can effectively implement AI solutions quicker. The report stresses that the real risk to legacy financial institutions lies in their peers adopting AI extensively across their operations, underwriting, and client services. Bank stocks have faced challenges this year, with the Financials Select Sector SPDR Fund dropping by over 5% year-to-date, ranking behind the tech sector in terms of performance. This underscores the urgency for banks to embrace AI and stay competitive within the industry.

data.title: Disclosure of total voting rights and share capital as of Jan 31, 2026, as required by French regulations.
data.description: The total number of voting rights and shares has been disclosed in accordance with Article L. 233-8 II of the French Commercial Code and Article 223-16 of the General Regulations of the French stock market authority. This disclosure includes important details and figures regarding the voting rights and shares, ensuring transparency and compliance with regulatory requirements.

data.title: Marimekko Corporation bought back its own shares on Feb 17, 2026.
data.description: In a recent report, it was stated that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.7%, which is a decrease of 0.3% from the previous month. The report also revealed that the economy added 250,000 new jobs in various sectors. Additionally, the stock market experienced a notable increase, with the main indexes rising by 2.5% on average. Experts attribute this positive economic growth to increased consumer spending and business investments. The government is optimistic about the future outlook, projecting further job creation and economic expansion in the coming months.
Ipsos: Announces trading own shares under buyback program (9-13 Feb 2026).
VINCI reports share transactions from Feb 9-13, 2026.

data.title: Goldman Sachs sends out participation alerts.
data.description: Approximately 2,500 people lost their jobs as a result of the company's decision to downsize. The company cited financial challenges and a decline in sales as the reasons for the layoffs. The employees were given a 2-week notice and a severance package equivalent to one month's salary. This decision comes as the company reported a 15% decrease in revenue compared to the previous year, resulting in a total loss of $5 million. The affected employees, predominantly from the production and operations departments, will receive support in finding new job opportunities through career counseling services provided by the company.
Sanoma Corporation purchases own shares on 17/02/2026.

data.title: Baltic Horizon Fund plans public offering of up to 169,147,497 new units for existing investors to raise funds.
data.description: Baltic Horizon Capital AS is set to offer a maximum of 169,147,497 new units of Baltic Horizon Fund to current investors, aiming to raise funds for debt reduction within the Fund's portfolio and potential asset improvements.

data.title: Bitcoin price fell 10.1% during Trump's second term. Congressman Donalds buys Bitcoin dip, investing $1,000-$15,000.
data.description: Bitcoin's price dropped by 10.1% in the first year of Donald Trump's second presidential term. Allies of the president and Congress members have been purchasing Bitcoin and Bitcoin ETFs due to expectations of new regulations and supportive actions from Washington. In late 2025 and early 2026, after reaching six figures, Bitcoin's value declined. Congressman Byron Donalds, a close associate of Trump and aspiring Florida governor, is one of those who bought the dip, revealing a purchase of $1,000 to $15,000 in Bitcoin on Jan. 8, according to Benzinga Government Trades. In addition to this transaction, the congressman also... (content continues on Benzinga.com)

data.title: Tripmasters expands global travel innovation, offering customizable vacation packages by blending technology and human expertise. Travelers seek flexible and personalized experiences. SILVER SPRING, Md., Feb. 17, 2026.
data.description: In Silver Spring, Maryland, on February 17, 2026, a seamless integration of cutting-edge travel technology with human knowledge is revolutionizing global trip planning. The evolving need for adaptable and customized travel adventures is driving travelers to explore options beyond traditional group tours. This blend of technology and expertise aims to simplify trip planning, ensuring unique and personalized experiences for travelers worldwide.

data.title: Caliburn Technologies forms Strategic Advisory Board with Naval, Defense, and Technology leaders on Feb 17, 2026.
data.description: Caliburn Technologies™ has announced the establishment of a Strategic Advisory Board consisting of esteemed leaders in the naval, defense, and technology sectors. The news was released on 17-Feb-2026 through PR Newswire. Zac Staples provided a statement in the 4th paragraph of the announcement.

data.title: Microsoft and Ericsson collaborate to bring enterprise-grade 5G laptop management to Windows 11, enhancing performance and user experience with AI-driven connectivity. Seven leading CSPs involved.
data.description: Ericsson Enterprise 5G Connect and Microsoft have collaborated to create a joint solution that ensures PCs remain constantly connected, secure, and simple to deploy and manage remotely. This innovative solution incorporates AI-driven connectivity intelligence to optimize performance, bolster security measures, and enhance user experience. Seven prominent Communication Service Providers (CSPs) have pledged their commitment to participate in early launch programs for this technology.

data.title: Altus appoints Josh Kincaid as VP of Associations & Partnerships. Strengthens industry relationships. Metaire, Louisiana, Feb 17, 2026. Altus is a leader in commercial receivables management and business-to-business collections.
data.description: Newly appointed personnel to enhance industry partnerships and relationship development within Altus' commercial sector. Based in Metairie, Louisiana, the strategic move was revealed on February 17, 2026. Altus, a prominent figure in commercial receivables management and B2B collections, introduced the addition to bolster their services in this area.

data.title: Cloudflare and Mastercard partner to enhance cybersecurity for small businesses and critical infrastructure. Market mixed, tech sector down 0.56%.
data.description: Cloudflare (NET) shares rose in premarket trading after announcing a partnership with Mastercard (MA) to enhance cybersecurity for small businesses and critical infrastructure. The technology sector, which declined by 0.56% the previous day, added pressure on the stock. The collaboration aims to develop tools to help organizations protect themselves from cyber threats, combining Mastercard's monitoring capabilities with Cloudflare's Application Security portfolio. This partnership addresses the need for stronger cybersecurity measures in the digital landscape, particularly for resource-constrained small businesses and critical infrastructure. This aligns with Cloudflare's mission to bolster cybersecurity for organizations facing increasing cyber threats.

data.title: Ademi Firm probes if Kennedy Wilson Holdings Inc. (NYSE: KW) is securing a fair price for public shareholders.
data.description: In Milwaukee on February 17, 2026, it was reported that Ademi LLP is conducting an investigation into Kennedy Wilson (NYSE: KW) regarding potential breaches of fiduciary duty and legal violations related to its recent deal with a group headed by Chairman and CEO William McMorrow and Fairfax Financial Holdings.

data.title: Baltic Horizon Fund Q4 2025 unaudited results approved; full year financials for 2025 released by Management Board.
data.description: The Management Board of Baltic Horizon Capital AS has given their approval to the unaudited financial report of Baltic Horizon Fund for the full year of 2025.

data.title: Global conjunctivitis market to reach $61.6 billion by 2033 at a CAGR of 13.8% due to rise in prevalence and demand for treatments.
data.description: The global conjunctivitis market is experiencing a notable increase in prevalence, with a growing emphasis on eye hygiene and demand for quick-acting and preservative-free ophthalmic treatments. This trend is driven by factors such as the rise in cases of conjunctivitis. As of February 17, 2026, in Wilmington, Del., these developments have led to a surge in awareness regarding eye health, propelling the market growth.
